1.o: Where there is the truth of grace, there will be an increase of it. The path of the just is as the shining light, which shines more and more unto the perfect day. And where there is the increase of grace, God must have all the glory. Where faith grows, love will abound, for faith works by love. It shows faith and patience, such as may be proposed as a pattern for others, when trials from God, and persecutions from men, quicken the exercise of those graces; for the patience and faith of which the apostle gloried, bore them up, and enabled them to endure all their tribulations.
1.m: When someone has true grace, it will grow stronger over time. A person who follows what is right becomes brighter and clearer each day. As grace increases, all the credit goes to God. When faith grows, love also gets bigger because faith leads to love. This shows strong faith and patience, which other people can look at as examples. Trials from God and troubles from others help these good qualities shine through. Faith and patience helped the apostles get through all their difficulties.

1.o: Religion, if worth anything, is worth every thing; and those have no religion, or none worth having, or know not how to value it, cannot find their hearts to suffer for it. We cannot by all our sufferings, any more than by our services, merit heaven; but by our patience under sufferings, we are prepared for the promised joy. Nothing more strongly marks a man for eternal ruin, than a spirit of persecution and enmity to the name and people of God. God will trouble those that trouble his people. And there is a rest for the people of God; a rest from sin and sorrow. The certainty of future recompence is proved by the righteousness of God. The thoughts of this should be terrible to wicked men, and support the righteous. Faith, looking to the great day, is enabled partly to understand the book of providence, which appears confused to unbelievers. The Lord Jesus will in that day appear from heaven. He will come in the glory and power of the upper world. His light will be piercing, and his power consuming, to all who in that day shall be found as chaff. This appearance will be terrible to those that know not God, especially to those who rebel against revelation, and obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ. This is the great crime of multitudes, the gospel is revealed, and they will not believe it; or if they pretend to believe, they will not obey it. Believing the truths of the gospel, is in order to our obeying the precepts of the gospel. Though sinners may be long spared, they will be punished at last. They did sin's work, and must receive sin's wages. Here God punishes sinners by creatures as instruments; but then, it will be destruction from the Almighty; and who knows the power of his anger? It will be a joyful day to some, to the saints, to those who believe and obey the gospel. In that bright and blessed day, Christ Jesus will be glorified and admired by his saints. And Christ will be glorified and admired in them. His grace and power will be shown, when it shall appear what he has purchased for, and wrought in, and bestowed upon those who believe in him. Lord, if the glory put upon thy saints shall be thus admired, how much more shalt thou be admired, as the Bestower of that glory! The glory of thy justice in the damnation of the wicked will be admired, but not as the glory of thy mercy in the salvation of believers. How will this strike the adoring angels with holy admiration, and transport thy admiring saints with eternal rapture! The meanest believer shall enjoy more than the most enlarged heart can imagine while we are here; Christ will be admired in all those that believe, the meanest believer not excepted.
1.m:
Religion that means anything is worth everything. People without real faith, or who don’t see its true value, won't suffer for it. Our suffering doesn’t earn us heaven, but it prepares us for the joy promised. A person who persecutes others shows they’re headed for eternal ruin. God will punish those who harm his followers. There’s peace waiting for God’s people—a place free from sin and sadness. Knowing God will reward us someday proves his fairness. Thinking about this should scare bad people and comfort good ones. Faith helps us understand God’s plan, even if it seems confusing to those who don’t believe. 

Jesus will return one day from heaven. He’ll come with great glory and power. For those who reject him, his presence will be terrifying. Unbelievers and rebels against God will fear him. Many refuse to follow the gospel, even though it’s been revealed to them. Some claim to believe but don’t obey. True belief leads to obedience. Sinners might escape punishment for a while, but eventually they’ll face justice. Those who do evil will get what they deserve. 

God uses different ways to punish wrongdoers now, but in the end, it will be total destruction from God himself. That’s scary to think about. But for believers, it will be a happy day. Jesus will be honored by his faithful followers. He’ll show how much he loves and strengthens those who trust in him.

Lord, if people will admire the glory given to your saints, how much more will you be admired as the one who gives that glory!

People will admire your justice in punishing sinners, but even more so your mercy in saving believers. This will amaze the angels and fill your saints with joy forever.

Even the humblest believer will experience more than anyone can imagine now. Christ will be seen in all his glory by everyone who believes, including the simplest follower.

1.o: Believing thoughts and expectations of the second coming of Christ should lead us to pray to God more, for ourselves and others. If there is any good in us, it is owing to the good pleasure of his goodness, and therefore it is called grace. There are many purposes of grace and good-will in God toward his people, and the apostle prays that God would complete in them the work of faith with power. This is to their doing every other good work. The power of God not only begins, but carries on the work of faith. And this is the great end and design of the grace of our God and Lord Jesus Christ, which is made known to us, and wrought in us.
1.m: Thinking about Jesus returning should encourage us to pray more, both for ourselves and others. Any good we have comes from God’s kindness, which we call grace. God has many plans for his people through his grace and good will. So the apostle asks God to help finish the work of faith in us with strength. This helps us do all kinds of good things. God’s power starts and continues the work of faith. This is what God’s grace and the work of Jesus Christ aim to do—showing us these things and making them real in us.

2.o: If errors arise among Christians, we should set them right; and good men will be careful to suppress errors which rise from mistaking their words and actions. We have a cunning adversary, who watches to do mischief, and will promote errors, even by the words of Scripture. Whatever uncertainty we are in, or whatever mistakes may arise about the time of Christ's coming, that coming itself is certain. This has been the faith and hope of all Christians, in all ages of the church; it was the faith and hope of the Old Testament saints. All believers shall be gathered together to Christ, to be with him, and to be happy in his presence for ever. We should firmly believe the second coming of Christ; but there was danger lest the Thessalonians, being mistaken as to the time, should question the truth or certainty of the thing itself. False doctrines are like the winds that toss the water to and fro; and they unsettle the minds of men, which are as unstable as water. It is enough for us to know that our Lord will come, and will gather all his saints unto him. A reason why they should not expect the coming of Christ, as at hand, is given. There would be a general falling away first, such as would occasion the rise of antichrist, that man of sin. There have been great disputes who or what is intended by this man of sin and son of perdition. The man of sin not only practises wickedness, but also promotes and commands sin and wickedness in others; and is the son of perdition, because he is devoted to certain destruction, and is the instrument to destroy many others, both in soul and body. As God was in the temple of old, and worshipped there, and is in and with his church now; so the antichrist here mentioned, is a usurper of God's authority in the Christian church, who claims Divine honours.
2.m:
If Christians make mistakes, we should correct them. Good people try hard to stop wrong ideas that come from misunderstanding what others say or do. Our enemy tries to cause trouble and uses parts of the Bible to spread these wrong ideas. Even if we're unsure about when Jesus will return, we can be sure that he will come someday. Believers through history have always had this hope. When Jesus comes back, he will bring all his followers to himself where they will live happily forever. 

We need to believe that Jesus will come again. But some people might doubt this because they misunderstand when it will happen. Wrong teachings confuse people just like wind makes water move around. These false ideas upset people’s minds. 

It's enough to know that Jesus will return and gather all his saints to him. One reason why we shouldn't think Jesus is coming soon is that there will be a big fall away from true belief first. This will lead to the rise of someone called the Antichrist, who causes much harm. People debate who this person is, but he does evil things and tells others to do evil too. He brings ruin on himself and others. Just as God used to be in the temple and is now with his church, this Antichrist takes over God's place in the church and demands worship for himself.

2.o: Something hindered or withheld the man of sin. It is supposed to be the power of the Roman empire, which the apostle did not mention more plainly at that time. Corruption of doctrine and worship came in by degrees, and the usurping of power was gradual; thus the mystery of iniquity prevailed. Superstition and idolatry were advanced by pretended devotion, and bigotry and persecution were promoted by pretended zeal for God and his glory. This mystery of iniquity was even then begun; while the apostles were yet living, persons pretended zeal for Christ, but really opposed him. The fall or ruin of the antichristian state is declared. The pure word of God, with the Spirit of God, will discover this mystery of iniquity, and in due time it shall be destroyed by the brightness of Christ's coming. Signs and wonders, visions and miracles, are pretended; but they are false signs to support false doctrines; and lying wonders, or only pretended miracles, to cheat the people; and the diabolical deceits with which the antichristian state has been supported, are notorious. The persons are described, who are his willing subjects. Their sin is this; They did not love the truth, and therefore did not believe it; and they were pleased with false notions. God leaves them to themselves, then sin will follow of course, and spiritual judgments here, and eternal punishments hereafter. These prophecies have, in a great measure, come to pass, and confirm the truth of the Scriptures. This passage exactly agrees with the system of popery, as it prevails in the Romish church, and under the Romish popes. But though the son of perdition has been revealed, though he has opposed and exalted himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; and has spoken and acted as if he were a god upon earth, and has proclaimed his insolent pride, and supported his delusions, by lying miracles and all kinds of frauds; still the Lord has not yet fully destroyed him with the brightness of his coming; that and other prophecies remain to be fulfilled before the end shall come.
2.m:
Something stopped the man of sin. People think it was the power of the Roman Empire, which the apostle didn't name clearly back then. Bad ideas and practices crept in slowly over time, and taking too much power happened little by little. So the mystery of evil grew stronger. Pretended devotion helped superstitions and idols spread, and fake concern for God led to bigoted and persecuting behavior. Even while the apostles were alive, some claimed to care about Jesus but actually worked against him.

The downfall of the anti-Christian state is predicted. The true Word of God, along with the Holy Spirit, will expose these wrong beliefs. When Jesus returns, their lies will be destroyed. False signs and wonders were used to support false teachings, tricking people with pretend miracles. The tricks the anti-Christian state used to fool people are well-known.

People who willingly followed this man of sin had one main problem: They didn’t love the truth, so they didn’t believe it. Instead, they liked false ideas. Once God lets them go, they naturally turn to sin, facing both earthly troubles and future punishment.

These predictions have mostly come true, proving the accuracy of the Scriptures. This fits perfectly with what the Catholic Church and its leaders do today. Though the son of destruction has shown himself, opposing and putting himself above everything else, acting like a god on Earth, and using deceitful miracles and scams to keep control, God hasn’t completely destroyed him yet with the light of Jesus' return. Other prophecies need to happen first before the end comes.

2.o: When we hear of the apostacy of many, it is a great comfort and joy, that there is a remnant according to the election of grace, which does and shall persevere; especially we should rejoice, if we have reason to hope that we are of that number. The preservation of the saints, is because God loved them with an everlasting love, from the beginning of the world. The end and the means must not be separated. Faith and holiness must be joined together as well as holiness and happiness. The outward call of God is by the gospel; and this is rendered effectual by the inward working of the Spirit. The belief of the truth brings the sinner to rely on Christ, and so to love and obey him; it is sealed by the Holy Spirit upon his heart. We have no certain proof of any thing having been delivered by the apostles, more than what we find contained in the Holy Scriptures. Let us then stand fast in the doctrines taught by the apostles, and reject all additions, and vain traditions.
2.m:
When we hear about many people turning away from God, it gives us comfort and joy to know that there is still a small group who will stay faithful. Especially if we think we might be part of that group, we should feel happy.

God has always loved those he chooses to save, and that’s why they keep going strong. We shouldn’t separate the goal from the way to get there. Faith and goodness need to go hand in hand, just like goodness and happiness do.

God calls people through the message of the gospel, and the Holy Spirit makes sure that call takes hold inside their hearts. Believing the truth helps sinners trust in Jesus, love him, and follow his commands. This faith is confirmed by the Holy Spirit within our hearts.

We don’t have any proof beyond the Bible for anything else the apostles said. So let’s stick to what the apostles taught in the Scriptures and avoid adding extra ideas or useless traditions.

2.o: We may and should direct our prayers, not only to God the Father, through our Lord Jesus Christ, but also to our Lord Jesus Christ himself. And we should pray in his name unto God, not only as his Father, but as our Father in and through him. The love of God in Christ Jesus, is the spring and fountain of all the good we have or hope for. There is good reason for strong consolations, because the saints have good hope through grace. The free grace and mercy of God are what they hope for, and what their hopes are founded on, and not any worth or merit of their own. The more pleasure we take in the word, and works, and ways of God, the more likely we shall be to persevere therein. But, if we are wavering in faith, and of a doubtful mind, halting and faltering in our duty, no wonder that we are strangers to the joys of religion.
2.m:
We can and should talk to God both as the Father, through Jesus, and directly to Jesus himself. We should ask things from God, not just as Jesus' Father, but as our Father too, through Jesus. 

The kindness of God shown through Jesus is where all the good things we receive come from. This gives us strong reasons to feel comforted. Our hope comes from God’s grace, not anything we’ve done ourselves.

The more we enjoy God’s teachings, actions, and plans, the better we will stick to them. But if we’re unsure about our faith and hesitate in doing what’s right, we won’t find joy in our religious life.

3.o: Those who are far apart still may meet together at the throne of grace; and those not able to do or receive any other kindness, may in this way do and receive real and very great kindness. Enemies to the preaching of the gospel, and persecutors of its faithful preachers, are unreasonable and wicked men. Many do not believe the gospel; and no wonder if such are restless and show malice in their endeavours to oppose it. The evil of sin is the greatest evil, but there are other evils we need to be preserved from, and we have encouragement to depend upon the grace of God. When once the promise is made, the performance is sure and certain. The apostle had confidence in them, but that was founded upon his confidence in God; for there is otherwise no confidence in man. He prays for them for spiritual blessings. It is our sin and our misery, that we place our affections upon wrong objects. There is not true love of God, without faith in Jesus Christ. If, by the special grace of God, we have that faith which multitudes have not, we should earnestly pray that we may be enabled, without reserve, to obey his commands, and that we may be enabled, without reserve, to the love of God, and the patience of Christ.
3.m:
People who are far away can still come together at the throne of grace. Even those who cannot help each other in other ways can give and receive real kindness here. People who hate the message about Jesus and hurt those who share it are being unfair and mean. 

Many people don’t believe the good news, so it’s no surprise they try hard to stop it. Sin causes the worst problems, but we need protection from all kinds of trouble. We can trust God will keep his promises.

The apostle believed in these people because he trusted God. Without God, we can't really count on anyone else. He prayed for them to get spiritual help. Our biggest problem is putting our hopes in the wrong things.

True love for God comes only through believing in Jesus. If God gives us the special gift of faith—something many don’t have—we should ask him to help us follow his rules completely and love him fully.

3.o: Those who have received the gospel, are to live according to the gospel. Such as could work, and would not, were not to be maintained in idleness. Christianity is not to countenance slothfulness, which would consume what is meant to encourage the industrious, and to support the sick and afflicted. Industry in our callings as men, is a duty required by our calling as Christians. But some expected to be maintained in idleness, and indulged a curious and conceited temper. They meddled with the concerns of others, and did much harm. It is a great error and abuse of religion, to make it a cloak for idleness or any other sin. The servant who waits for the coming of his Lord aright, must be working as his Lord has commanded. If we are idle, the devil and a corrupt heart will soon find us somewhat to do. The mind of man is a busy thing; if it is not employed in doing good, it will be doing evil. It is an excellent, but rare union, to be active in our own business, yet quiet as to other people's. If any refused to labour with quietness, they were to note him with censure, and to separate from his company, yet they were to seek his good by loving admonitions. The Lords is with you while you are with him. Hold on your way, and hold on to the end. We must never give over, or tire in our work. It will be time enough to rest when we come to heaven.
3.m:
People who believe in the gospel should follow its teachings. Those who can work but won’t shouldn't expect to be supported without doing anything. 

Christianity doesn’t allow laziness because that would take away help meant for hardworking people and those who are sick or suffering. As Christians, we need to work diligently at our jobs.

Some people thought they didn’t need to work and just wanted to spend their time however they pleased. This caused trouble for others. Using religion as an excuse for laziness or any kind of sin is wrong.

A true Christian worker will always be busy following God’s commands. If we’re lazy, the devil and our sinful nature will find ways to keep us busy with bad things. Our minds naturally want to be active, so if we don’t use them for good, they’ll get involved in evil.

It’s best to focus on our own tasks and stay out of other people’s affairs. When someone refuses to work quietly, they should be gently warned about their behavior. 

God stays close to us as long as we stay faithful. Keep going and don’t stop until the very end. Don’t ever give up on your duties. There will be plenty of time to rest once we reach heaven.

3.o: The apostle prays for the Thessalonians. And let us desire the same blessings for ourselves and our friends. Peace with God. This peace is desired for them always, or in every thing. Peace by all means; in every way; that, as they enjoyed the means of grace, they might use all methods to secure peace. We need nothing more to make us safe and happy, nor can we desire any thing better for ourselves and our friends, than to have God's gracious presence with us and them. No matter where we are, if God be with us; nor who is absent, if God be present. It is through the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that we hope to have peace with God, and to enjoy the presence of God. This grace is all in all to make us happy; though we wish ever so much to others, there remains enough for ourselves.
3.m:
The apostle prayed for the Thessalonians. Let’s pray for the same things for ourselves and our loved ones. 

Peace with God. He wanted them to always have peace, in everything. They should seek peace by all means, using every method available. Having God’s presence makes us safe and happy. Wherever we are, having God with us is what matters most. Even if other people aren’t around, God’s presence is enough.

We hope to have peace with God because of the grace of Jesus. God’s grace gives us everything we need to be truly happy. Even if we wish good things for others, there will still be plenty left over for ourselves.
